frameworks.name: rate-distortion theory
frameworks.one_line: Formalizes memory as a channel trading information rate against distortion

frameworks.name: neural population coding
frameworks.one_line: Population code realizes the computational rate-distortion objective

core_question: How can rate-distortion theory of memory be realized by a neural population coding model?
key_assumptions: Memory operates as a channel with bounded information rate
Errors carry a distortion cost that the system minimizes
Population coding can implement the rate-distortion tradeoff
manipulations: Mapped abstract rate-distortion framework onto a population coding model
Reanalyzed monkey prefrontal recordings during an oculomotor delayed response task
key_findings: Model reproduces key regularities of visual working memory
Model accounts for regularities not previously explained by population coding
A novel model prediction was verified in prefrontal recordings
interpretations: Rate-distortion theory can be biologically realized through neural population codes
Working memory regularities reflect an information-rate vs distortion tradeoff
